title: Randomization (question order, matrix-row order, choice order)
description: questionsOrder: 'random' on a page shuffles its questions per respondent; rowsOrder: 'random' on a matrix shuffles its row sub-questions; choicesOrder: 'random' on a single/multi-select/ranking shuffles its options. The order each respondent actually saw is captured at submission time — shown_questions in render order, shown_choice_orders for randomized choices — and threads to fact_response_item.display_order (design doc §3.5 'Randomization and display-order capture'; never reconstructed in SQL, §4.11). Block-aware randomization is structural, not a separate flag: a reusable scale fits naturally as a matrix tagged with construct_block (the tag inherits to row sub-questions per §3.5 'Construct membership'), so PHQ-9 items shuffle among themselves and stay contiguous because the matrix is the boundary. visibleIf reads values, not display positions, so within-page / within-matrix / within-question randomization is always safe to combine with branching. Static-panel and survey-level page shuffling aren't wired today (deferred, design doc §5).

pages.name: p_warmup
pages.title: Warm-up questions (randomized)
pages.description: questionsOrder: 'random' on a page shuffles the page's elements per respondent. The two questions here are unrelated, so order is the only thing that changes.

elements.name: phq9
elements.title: Over the last two weeks, how often have you been bothered by…
elements.description: Matrix with rowsOrder: 'random' is the block-aware-randomization shape: each row is its own sub-question (stable_name 'phq9.q1', 'phq9.q2', 'phq9.q3'), the shared response scale lives in columns, and the rows shuffle per respondent. construct_block on the matrix inherits to the row sub-questions in dim_question (design doc §3.5 'Construct membership' inheritance rule), so each row carries construct_block='phq9'. construct_item is leaf-only — set per row, not on the container.

rows.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling/staying asleep, or sleeping too much

elements.name: favorite_color
elements.title: Pick a favorite color
elements.description: choicesOrder: 'random' shuffles the options shown to this respondent. The chosen option_key is unchanged — selection is by value, not position — so dim_option.display_order (the authored position) and the per-respondent rendered position are different things. The per-respondent rendered choice order lives in raw_responses.shown_choice_orders; a fact-side choice_display_order column is deferred (design doc §5 'Choice-order analysis surface').

elements.name: color_followup
elements.title: Is that the color of something you own?
elements.description: visibleIf reads the chosen value ({favorite_color}), not its rendered position, so choices-order randomization never affects branching reachability. The publish gate's round-trip oracle treats randomized choices as order-invariant for the same reason (design doc §3.6).
